The '''''Captor''-class heavy munitions cruiser''', also known as the '''Trade Federation heavy munitions cruiser''', was a type of commercial freighter-[[Warship/Legends|warship]] used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] to transport [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] to buyers throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] around [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]].<ref name="SWR:TSHW" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:Trade_fed_freighter_shw.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Trade Federation freighter engines]]

They shared similar designs with the [[LH-3010 capital freighter]] and [[Lucrehulk-class LH-3210 cargo freighter/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class LH-3210]] freighters also used by the Federation, and were at least several hundred meters long.<ref name="SWR:TSHW" />
Although built as dedicated [[Warship/Legends|warships]] for the corporation's Trade Defense arm, they were much smaller and less powerful than the ''Lucrehulk''-class, which the Trade Federation later converted into battleships for their attack on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] and used during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="RP" />

The heavy munitions cruiser had large hangars located at the center of its superstructure, where fighter squadrons could launch into battle in wartime. It was propelled by three large thrusters.<ref name="SWR:TSHW" />
Like many of the Trade Federation's ships, a heavy munitions cruiser depended heavily on automated systems.<ref name="RP" />

==History==
[[File:Trade_Fed_Bacta_Ship.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A Federation freighter attacked by the [[Stark Commercial Combine|Stark Combine]]]]
The ''Captor''-class was part of the [[Trade Defense Force]] around [[214 BBY]]. During that time, a series of [[Transport/Legends|transport]] businesses in the [[Rseik sector/Legends|Rseik sector]] decided to join the Trade Federation. They were burdened by pirates, and the Defense Force sent a [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] including a cruiser and two [[Munifex-class light cruiser|''Munifex''-class cruisers]] to stop the pirates.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

During the bacta drought leading to the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]], bacta ships of this type were attacked by warships utilized by the [[Stark Commercial Combine]]. As a result of these attacks, the Trade Federation demanded the right to build up its military forces or stop trade altogether.<ref name="SWR:TSHW" />

During the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]], freighter/cruisers of this kind were used to transport [[Judicial Department/Legends|Judicial]] and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] forces (including [[Red Squadron (Galactic Republic)/Legends|Red]] and [[Blue Squadron (Jedi Order)|Blue Squadrons]]) to the [[Fifth Battle of Qotile]].<ref name="SWR:TSHW" /><ref name="TEGTW" />

Perhaps the best-known vessel of this class, ''[[Admiral Korvin]]'', was used by [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sienar]] for his [[Second Battle of Zonama Sekot|mission]] to [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] in [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]], equipped with 3,000 [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s, a force of at least 30 [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|''Vulture'' droid starfighters]], 3 [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 landing craft]], and a contingent of heavily-armored [[E-5 battle droid]]s, a new [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] model developed after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]. Nevertheless, ''Admiral Korvin'' also carried a hundred [[Human/Legends|human]] troops, and a two-seater [[Spy ship/Legends|Spy ship]] which required a living pilot. It served within the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|Outland Regions Security Force]] and, under orders from [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], would attack Zonama Sekot.<ref name="RP" />
